title: crepis sancta subsp. obovata
intro: Crepis sancta subsp. obovata, also known as the holy hawk's beard or obovate hawk's beard, is a subspecies of plant in the dandelion family that is native to Europe and Asia. This plant is used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Crepis sancta subsp. obovata include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and support immune system function. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea or infused oil, which can be used to treat various health conditions, but more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
